# infisical_webhook (Resource)

Create and manage webhooks in Infisical. Webhooks notify an external URL when secrets change at a given path.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    infisical = {
      # version = <latest version>
      source = "infisical/infisical"
    }
  }
}

provider "infisical" {
  host = "https://app.infisical.com" # Only required if using self hosted instance of Infisical, default is https://app.infisical.com
  auth = {
    universal = {
      client_id     = "<machine-identity-client-id>"
      client_secret = "<machine-identity-client-secret>"
    }
  }
}

# Notify an external endpoint (e.g. an AWS Lambda) when secrets change
resource "infisical_webhook" "lambda_trigger" {
  project_id         = "<your-project-id>"
  environment        = "prod"
  secret_path        = "/"
  webhook_url        = "https://example.com/infisical-webhook"
  webhook_secret_key = "<signing-key>" # Optional: used to sign the payload so the receiver can verify it
  events_filter      = ["secrets.modified"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `environment` (String) The slug of the environment the webhook listens to.
- `project_id` (String) The ID of the project the webhook belongs to.
- `webhook_url` (String, Sensitive) The URL Infisical sends the event payload to.

### Optional

- `events_filter` (Set of String) The events that trigger the webhook. Options: secrets.modified, secrets.rotation-failed. An empty set means the webhook fires on every supported event.
- `is_disabled` (Boolean) Whether the webhook is disabled. Defaults to false.
- `secret_path` (String) The secret path the webhook listens to. Defaults to '/'.
- `type` (String) The webhook type. Options: general, slack, microsoft-teams. Defaults to 'general'.
- `webhook_secret_key` (String, Sensitive) The secret key used to sign the webhook payload so the receiver can verify it. Write-only: it is never returned by the API, so it cannot be imported.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the webhook.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import infisical_webhook.example <webhook_id>
```
